name: pcs-tools
description: PCS Tools internal Claude Code plugins for the Kit Builder, MAP pricing, and Jira-task-builder workflows.

plugins.name: pcs-promo-parser
plugins.description: Parse vendor promo decks (Milwaukee, DeWalt, Makita, Bosch, EGO, Flex, GearWrench, Crescent) into PCS Kit Builder Stage 1 outputs. v1.6.0: DeWalt-run correctness — multi-paid bundle members and every included slot now emit an explicit 0.00 Price (never blank), bundle total on slot 1, so kb build-imports and decode-formula stop erroring/garbling on blank prices; the bundle anchor (slot 1) is the main/highest-value tool regardless of which source row held the price; the DeWalt SKU regex admits bare corded DW#### SKUs (DW735/DW7350/DW735X); DeWalt PMAPP = IN-STORE ONLY (and the footer ADVERTISING WINDOW: IN-STORE ONLY) is a per-SKU brick-and-mortar exclusion, and in-store/volume/spend-threshold BMSM with no online window routes to Non-Included instead of Other-Promotions (never a Jira task); the repeated DeWalt confidentiality footer is ignored in new-product/brick-mortar triage; and the verification grep searches the whole multi-page promo range (not just the lead page) so continuation-page SKUs stop being falsely held image-only. v1.5.0: kit outputs (Promo-List/RSA-Kits) gain a trailing `Page` column and the parser saves each rendered data page to deck_pages/p###.png, so the Jira builder can attach the source deck image and read POS/credit clues (kb ignores the extra column). v1.4.0: robust + selective PDF ingestion (PyMuPDF in-process render when the native rasteriser is missing — no poppler; cover/marketing pages skipped from the vision read for big token savings, no accuracy loss); the Milwaukee SKU pattern now admits sub-brand forms (MX Fuel, letter-infix, bare, Empire, Stiletto, apparel, layout) so legit SKUs stop being held to For-Review; ambiguous split battery slides ask the operator to confirm pairing before holding. v1.3.0: a per-SKU + per-price verification gate independently re-grounds every SKU/price in the deck before writing (deterministic text grep + an adversarial verifier subagent + a vendor-regex check) and HOLDS anything ungroundable out of the Promo-List (For-Review `unverified`) — hallucinated SKUs/prices can't reach NetSuite. v1.2.0: new `Parsed Decks/<Vendor>/<session>/Promo Parsed Output/` folder tree; BMSM, e-rebate, and promo-code promos are parsed into a combined Other-Promotions.csv instead of excluded; a conditional For-Review.xlsx flags low-confidence + missing-data pages; empty outputs aren't written (Parser-Audit.csv is the always-present manifest).

plugins.name: pcs-map-updater
plugins.description: Merge a vendor MAP sheet with a NetSuite ERP export, flag exceptions, and export a review-ready Excel workbook for the monthly MAP update workflow.

plugins.name: pcs-jira-task-builder
plugins.description: Beta. Reads pcs-promo-parser Stage 1 CSVs (Promo Parsed Output/ folder) and creates matching Jira Tasks in the PCS Promotions space. Hard PROM write-protection (literal-phrase gate), defaults to PAT, per-vendor period naming, RSA per-row review, storefront link scaffolding. v0.4.0: titles corrected to PROM convention -- period token = P<N> where N is the deck's quarter number (Q3 -> 2026 P3, not a half-year P2; EGO stays H1/H2, Flex stays Q); the deck promo identifier is KEPT at the end of the title, bracketed ([PCR: P-00208522] / [PCE: 262776]) and omitted on no-ID / consolidated multi-ID tasks; titles generalized to short title-cased phrases with no vendor prefix, no SKUs, no ALL-CAPS / BUY (n)...GET (n) boilerplate; an online-only gate skips any non-online-executable Other-Promotions group (Online Execution No + no online window -- in-store / spend-threshold / volume, e.g. DeWalt PMAPP = IN-STORE ONLY) so it never becomes a Task (RSA stays the tracked exception); field mapping states plainly that PAT is a sandbox whose fields, option labels, and option IDs do NOT match PROM (PAT POS/Online option is labeled 'Option 1'; Promo Deck URL absent on PAT) -- discover fields/options at runtime, skip-and-flag what is missing. v0.3.0: Promo Type by a shared rule (free goods -> Manufacturer Free Goods; NLP -> Manufacturer NLP; coupon/BMSM/buy-X-get-Y -> Manufacturer Coupon; e-rebate -> E-rebate; self-funding left to a coworker; Buy-In is Think-Tank-only, never auto-defaulted); Online Execution = Yes when online dates exist; due = last takedown; POS Redemption = Yes for RSA/credit or a deck-page credit/redemption clue (any vendor); all non-RSA NLPs for a vendor/quarter consolidate into one Task with a sub-task per date group (start-pricing CSV + revert-schedule CSV, revert prices from NetSuite later); deck-page screenshot attached per task; Promo Deck URL set from a Google Drive hub search; Jira attachment token read from a project-folder text file (never chat); auto-HERO removed (no Priority bump / no (HERO) suffix); labels = Vendor + Quarter-Year (e.g. Q3-2026) + promo-type label(s) (Kit-Promo/NLP/Coupon/E-Rebate; +BMSM with Coupon; +RSA); WRITE TO PROM gate unchanged. v0.2.0: also creates Tasks from the parser's Other-Promotions.csv (BMSM / e-rebate / promo-code) with per-group review and BMSM/E-Rebate/Coupon labels; those three are no longer Non-Included reasons; tolerates absent empty files via the Parser-Audit manifest.

plugins.name: pcs-promo-creation
plugins.description: Guided end-to-end promo pipeline: parse a vendor deck -> build NetSuite kit imports -> create Jira promotion tasks, with a Yes/No confirmation before each stage and the hard PROM write-protection at the Jira step. Surfaces a drag-and-drop file-upload artifact at every document step (deck, cheat sheet, NetSuite export). An AI validation pass scans each stage's imports/exports before its gate, auto-correcting safe issues and flagging the rest. v1.17.0: the kit-image step is copy-paste-foolproof -- the Windows command leads with the PowerShell call operator (the & token) and a quoted exe path so it runs even when the session path has a space (the bare command failed on first run), with dead-simple open-PowerShell / open-Terminal steps; the NS export is read utf-8-sig first (CP1252 fallback) with mojibake stripped; kit titles/descriptions title-case ALL-CAPS source text, use a short product noun for free goods / bundle members (not the full Display Name), derive KEY FEATURES from the anchor's prose when it has no bullet list, and lead the title with the bundle's main tool (pairs with the parser bundle-anchor fix); and a kit-stage note flags whole-row decode-formula output as a blank Promo-List price to fix upstream. v1.16.0: kit stage pulls the master existing-kit list from a team Google Sheet -> build-imports --existing-kits (replace; bundled-snapshot fallback); macOS gets a prebuilt kb-macos binary fetched like kb.exe (floor >= 0.5.24); new final step confirms NetSuite build + offers to refresh the existing-kit list from a fresh NS Promo Kit Support search (append-merge, written back to the Sheet). v1.15.0: requires kb.exe >= 0.5.23 (the choose-N duplicate-kit fix); Step 0's version gate re-fetches it for operators still on 0.5.22 (kb.exe doesn't auto-update). v1.14.0: kit titles tightened — the Page Title cap is reconciled to 90 soft / 95 hard everywhere (removing the stale 80-char self-checks that forced over-truncation), plus descriptor-sourcing precedence (NS Display Name then Page Title then Name, landing on a specific product noun, never a bare 'Tool'), an anti-over-truncation rule, worked examples, and a per-title self-check. v1.13.0: when the NS export shows SKUs not yet built in NetSuite, the kit stage captures a resume kit — the unbuilt-SKU list, an unbuilt DECODE (copy-paste artifact), and a separate Unbuilt-SKU Promo-List of just the affected kits, plus a README — saved to the session folder so a later/separate chat can pick those kits up once the items are built. v1.12.0: the NetSuite kit stage surfaces the Promo Kit Support saved-search link (account 855722, search 16021) in both the Step 3 chat text and the DECODE copy-paste artifact, so the operator clicks straight through to run the search. v1.11.0: token discovery is robust — it finds the GitHub token in a subfolder or oddly-named file and extracts github_pat_/ghp_ by shape instead of assuming a GITHUB_TOKEN= line, and the non-Windows (Cowork Linux sandbox) source install is fixed (token-injected pip URL, drop winget). v1.10.0: Step 0 settles the Kit Builder up front — if `kb.exe` is missing and a `.env` token is present it installs it before parsing (no longer deferred to the kit stage); if no token is found it tells the operator to get the GitHub token `.env` from their admin and offers to continue without kit building (deck parsing + Jira still run; the NetSuite kit stages are skipped). v1.9.0: the Kit Builder ships as a prebuilt headless `kb.exe` — Step 0 checks `kb.exe --version` (>= 0.5.22) and on Windows Claude fetches it from the private Release via the `.env` token (no Python/pip/Git; source pip install stays as the dev/non-Windows fallback). v1.8.0: Stage-1 validation enforces the parser's verification gate — re-matches every emitted SKU to the vendor regex, asserts no held SKU leaked into an output, reconciles SKUs Held, and surfaces verification-held items at Gate 2. v1.7.0: requires kb >= 0.5.21 (collapses duplicate kit members sharing an Item ID); the Step 0 prereq check uses `pip show pcs-kit-builder-lite` (no `kb --version`). v1.6.0: the DECODE formula is shown as a copy-paste artifact (one Copy button per block) in chat. v1.5.0: a run lands in one session folder `Parsed Decks/<Vendor>/<session>/` with Promo Parsed Output / NetSuite Import Files / Images subfolders; the upload widget is recreated on every file (re)prompt; the parser's For-Review workbook is surfaced at the kit gate. v1.4.0: kit images are composed locally via a `kb --images-only` one-liner (macOS + Windows) since Cowork's sandbox can't reach NetSuite's image host. Orchestrates pcs-promo-parser and pcs-jira-task-builder and drives the Kit Builder `kb.exe` CLI binary (>= 0.5.24); Claude writes each kit's Page Title and Detailed Description from the item data using the ported title/description rules; prompts for deck, pricing cheat sheet, and NetSuite Promo Kit Support export uploads when needed.

plugins.name: pcs-facet-enrichment-pipeline
plugins.description: Anglera Faceted Search Backfill -> coverage gap analysis -> HIGH-confidence PDP backfill (real source PDPs, strict v15 dropdown validation, frequency-gated dropdown-addition proposals) -> NetSuite-ready facet import. Operator drops the export, names the brand, and double-clicks a one-click runner; Claude automates gap analysis, scraper config, QA, and NetSuite formatting. v1.1.0: only the final NetSuite import CSV is uploaded to the shared Google Drive NS-import folder; every other output (gap report, backfill plan, PDP-filled xlsx/log, QA summary, dropdown-additions list) stays in the operator's local working folder. v1.2.0: single-value enforcement — only safety_rating, drive_size, and battery_compatibility may hold multiple comma-joined values; the NetSuite-format step now collapses every other facet (incl. Anglera pass-through fields like For Use On / For Use With / Material) to its first value, protecting numeric thousands separators (10,000 cps stays whole), and the scraper's multi gate is an explicit allowlist so a MULTI_SELECT mislabel in attributes (NN).csv can't leak multi-values. v1.3.0: adds an in-chat manual-review gate — the scraper HOLDS uncertain values (retailer-sourced, normalization-adjusted, inferred-from-prose, out-of-dropdown, or multiple-candidate) in a review queue instead of writing them; the operator accepts/rejects each in an interactive chat artifact (default Accept, Accept-all/Reject-all), and only AUTO + accepted values are built into the NetSuite import (accepted out-of-dropdown values also logged operator-approved). Replaces the old pre-NetSuite Yes/No gate.

plugins.name: pcs-categorization
plugins.description: Autonomous weekly product categorization for the Toolup multi-stores. Refreshes each store's FULL collection vocabulary (nav + off-nav floating collections); dual-tree stores (Shop-by-Category + Shop-by-Brand, e.g. MTS) tag products in BOTH trees, plus a non-exclusive battery-platform tree. Reads existing tags first, classifies New Item V2 non-kit products to their bottom-most collection(s) + ancestor tags, removes New Item V2 when complete, adds CL-categorized. Add-only: the only tag ever removed is New Item V2. No-zero-tags guarantee via a fallback ladder; promo/operational collections excluded. Each run produces a color-coded Excel report (Store, Shopify ID, Handle, Variant SKU, Title, Vendor, category tree logic, proposed tags, 0-100 confidence red/yellow/green; weekly = one tab per store) delivered to a Google-Drive-for-Desktop synced folder. Runs gatelessly on a schedule. Write scope is exactly three: Shopify product tags, a scoped git push of lessons/change-reports to this repo, and the Drive project folder — everything else read-only. Per-store AND project lessons grow over time and auto-commit to the plugin repo each weekly run with a dated change report. Genuinely ambiguous items -> review queue (local + small Drive Doc). Bundles a read-only Python engine (owner-approved exception to the markdown-only rule).
